content.summaryText: Gestational Diabetes Monitoring in Prenatal Care Pathways focuses on risk-based prenatal follow-up and maternal-fetal monitoring. Primary objective: Interpret OGTT results.

content.questionText: In Gestational Diabetes Monitoring, which action best supports 'Interpret OGTT results'?
content.explanation: Correct answers prioritize patient safety, reassessment, and timely escalation.

options.text: Confirm gestational age, vitals, and baseline screening
Missing early warning signs between scheduled visits
Delay reassessment until symptoms are severe
Use treatment decisions without baseline documentation

content.summaryText: Immediate priority: confirm gestational age, vitals, and baseline screening. Practical emphasis: Set glucose targets.

content.questionText: Escalation is required when hypertension, bleeding, reduced fetal movement, or severe symptoms.
content.explanation: Early escalation reduces preventable deterioration and delays in definitive care.

content.sentence:
Start with <1> to establish baseline status.
Use <2> to deliver standardized intervention.
Document <3> to support continuity and quality review.
